name: ns
description: The NativeScript CLI lets you create, build, and deploy NativeScript based apps on iOS and Android devices

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Open the CLI's documentation in your web browser

options.description: Show help for the help subcommand

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Displays version information about the NativeScript CLI, core modules, and runtimes

options.description: Show help for the info subcommand

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates the project with the latest versions of platform runtimes, cross-platform modules and webpack

subcommands.name: next
subcommands.description: The latest development release

options.description: Show help for the update subcommand

args.name: version
args.description: The version to update the project to

subcommands.name: package-manager
subcommands.description: Prints the value of the current package manager

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Prints the value of the current package manager

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Enables the specified package manager for the NativeScript CLI. Supported values are npm, yarn and pnpm

options.description: Show help for the package-manager subcommand

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: Checks your system for configuration problems which might prevent the NativeScript CLI from working properly for the specified platform, if configured

subcommands.name: android
subcommands.description: Check your system configuration for android

subcommands.name: ios
subcommands.description: Check your system configuration for ios

options.description: Show help for the doctor subcommand

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: Migrates the app dependencies to a form compatible with NativeScript 6.0

options.description: Show help for the migrate subcommand

subcommands.name: usage-reporting
subcommands.description: Configures anonymous usage reporting for the NativeScript CLI. All data gathered is used strictly for improving the product and will never be used to identify or contact you

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Shows the current configuration for anonymous usage reporting for the NativeScript CLI

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: Enables anonymous usage reporting

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: Disables anonymous usage reporting

options.description: Show help for the usage-reporting subcommand

subcommands.name: error-reporting
subcommands.description: Configures anonymous error reporting for the NativeScript CLI. All data gathered is used strictly for improving the product and will never be used to identify or contact you

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Shows the current configuration for anonymous error reporting for the NativeScript CLI

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: Enables anonymous error reporting

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: Disables anonymous error reporting

options.description: Show help for the error-reporting subcommand

subcommands.name: proxy
subcommands.description: Displays the current proxy settings of the NativeScript CLI

subcommands.name: clear
subcommands.description: Clears the currently configured proxy settings of the NativeScript CLI

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets the proxy settings of the NativeScript CLI

options.description: Allows insecure SSL connections and transfers to be performed. In case your proxy doesn't have a CA certificate or has an invalid one you need to use this flag

args.name: proxy url
args.description: Full url of the proxy. For example, http://127.0.0.1:8888. If you do not provide the url when running the command, the NativeScript CLI will prompt you to provide it

options.description: Show help for the proxy subcommand

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new Nativescript project. Press Enter for an interactive walkthrough

options.description: Create a project using a predefined template
Create a project using the Angular template
Create a project using the React template
Create a project using the Vue template
Create a project using the Svelte template
Create a project using plain TypeScript
Create a project using plain JavaScript
Specifies the directory where you want to create the project, if different from the current directory. <directory> is the absolute path to an empty directory in which you want to create the project

Sets the application identifier of your project. <appid> is the value of the application identifier and it must meet the specific requirements of each platform that you want to target. If not specified, the application identifier is set to org.nativescript.<Project Name>. The application identifier must be a domain name in reverse
Show help for the create subcommand

args.name: application name
args.description: The name of the Nativescript project

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Clean your Nativescript project

options.description: Show help for the clean subcommand

subcommands.name: preview
subcommands.description: Produces a QR code which can be used to preview the app on a device

options.description: Skips the application compatibility checks and forces npm i to ensure all dependencies are installed
Disables Hot Module Replacement (HMR)
Show help for the preview subcommand

subcommands.name: platform
subcommands.description: Lists all platforms that the project currently targets. You can build and deploy your project only for these target platforms

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists all platforms that the project currently targets. You can build and deploy your project only for these target platforms

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Configures the current project to target the selected platform

subcommands.name: android
subcommands.description: The latest android platform

options.description: Sets the path to a NativeScript runtime for the specified platform that you want to use instead of the default runtime. <File Path> must point to a valid npm package

subcommands.name: android@[Version]
subcommands.description: The defined android platform eg. 1.0.0

options.description: Sets the path to a NativeScript runtime for the specified platform that you want to use instead of the default runtime. <File Path> must point to a valid npm package

subcommands.name: ios
subcommands.description: The latest ios platform

options.description: Sets the path to a NativeScript runtime for the specified platform that you want to use instead of the default runtime. <File Path> must point to a valid npm package

subcommands.name: ios@[Version]
subcommands.description: The defined ios platform eg. 1.0.0

options.description: Sets the path to a NativeScript runtime for the specified platform that you want to use instead of the default runtime. <File Path> must point to a valid npm package

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Removes the selected platform from the platforms that the project currently targets

subcommands.name: android
subcommands.description: The latest android platform

subcommands.name: ios
subcommands.description: The latest ios platform

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates the NativeScript runtime for the specified platform

subcommands.name: android
subcommands.description: The latest android platform

subcommands.name: ios
subcommands.description: The latest ios platform

options.description: Show help for the platform subcommand

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Runs your project on all connected devices or in native emulators for the selected platform

subcommands.name: android
subcommands.description: The latest android platform

options.description: Creates an Ahead-Of-Time build (Angular only)
Provides basic obfuscation and smaller app size
Creates a Webpack report inside a /report folder in the root folder
Creates inline source maps
Creates sources maps in the root folder (useful for Crashlytics usage with bundled app in release)
Prints verbose logs and the internal config before building
Enable production mode (will minify the code)
Add file replacement rules. For source files (.js and .ts) this will add a new alias to the config, for everything else, this will add a new copy rule
Disables Hot Module Replacement (HMR)
Skips the application compatibility checks and forces npm i to ensure all dependencies are installed
Specifies a connected device/emulator to start and run the app
Forces rebuilding the native application
Specifies that you want to debug the app in an emulator
Sets the number of seconds that the NativeScript CLI will wait for the debugger to boot. If not set, the default timeout is 90 seconds
Does not print the application output in the console
Produces a release build by running webpack in production mode and native build in release mode. Otherwise, produces a debug build
Bundle the application
Specifies that the command will produce and deploy an Android App Bundle
Creates a V8 Snapshot decreasing the app start time (only for release builds for Android)
Compiles the static assets produced by --env.snapshot into .so files allowing the native build to split them per architecture. This will reduce the app size when using the --aab option
Allows passing additional App Components for android
Specifies the file path to the keystore file (P12) which you want to use to code sign your APK
Provides the password for the keystore file specified with --key-store-path
Provides the alias for the keystore file specified with --key-store-path
Provides the password for the alias specified with --key-store-alias-password

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: Initiates a debugging session for your project on a connected device or native emulator

subcommands.name: android
subcommands.description: Start a debugging session for your project on a connected Android device or Android emulator

options.description: Creates an Ahead-Of-Time build (Angular only)
Provides basic obfuscation and smaller app size
Creates a Webpack report inside a /report folder in the root folder
Creates inline source maps
Creates sources maps in the root folder (useful for Crashlytics usage with bundled app in release)
Prints verbose logs and the internal config before building

Forces rebuilding the native application
Specifies that you want to debug the app in an emulator
Sets the number of seconds that the NativeScript CLI will wait for the debugger to boot. If not set, the default timeout is 90 seconds
Prepares, builds and deploys the application package on a device or in an emulator, and stops at the first JavaScript line until either the debugger frontend connects or a 30 seconds timeout elapses
Attaches the debug tools to a deployed and running app
Changes in your code will not be livesynced
Specifies that the command will produce and deploy an Android App Bundle
Creates a V8 Snapshot decreasing the app start time (only for release builds for Android)
Compiles the static assets produced by --env.snapshot into .so files allowing the native build to split them per architecture. This will reduce the app size when using the --aab option
Allows passing additional App Components for android

options.description: Sets the unit testing framework to install

subcommands.name: plugin
subcommands.description: Manage the plugins for your project

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Installs the specified plugin and its dependencies

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Uninstalls the specified plugin and its dependencies

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Uninstalls and installs the specified plugin(s) and its dependencies

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Builds the Android parts of a NativeScript plugin

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Creates a project for building a new NativeScript plugin

options.description: Specifies the directory where you want to create the project, if different from the current directory
Specifies the GitHub username, which will be used to build the URLs in the plugin's package.json file
Used to set the default file and class names in the plugin source
Specifies if a TypeScript demo should be created
Specifies if TypeScript demo should NOT be created
Specifies if an Angular demo should be created
Specifies if Angular demo should NOT be created
Specifies the custom seed archive, which you want to use to create your plugin
Show help for the plugin subcommand

subcommands.name: resources
subcommands.description: Manage the plugins for your project

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates the App_Resources's internal folder structure to conform to that of an Android Studio project

options.description: Sets the background color of the splashscreen

args.description: Path to an image that will be used to generate all splashscreens

args.description: Path to an image that will be used to generate all splashscreens

subcommands.name: prepare
subcommands.description: Starts a Webpack compilation and prepares the app's App_Resources and the plugins platforms directories

subcommands.description: Prepares your project for an Android build

options.description: Enables the hot module replacement (HMR) feature
Skips the application compatibility checks and forces npm i to ensure all dependencies are installed

options.description: Enables the hot module replacement (HMR) feature

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Builds the project for iOS and produces an APP or IPA that you can manually deploy in the iOS Simulator or on a device

options.description: Creates an Ahead-Of-Time build (Angular only)
Provides basic obfuscation and smaller app size
Creates a Webpack report inside a /report folder in the root folder
Creates inline source maps
Creates sources maps in the root folder (useful for Crashlytics usage with bundled app in release)
Prints verbose logs and the internal config before building
Enable production mode (will minify the code)
Add file replacement rules. For source files (.js and .ts) this will add a new alias to the config, for everything else, this will add a new copy rule
Produces a release build by running webpack in production mode and native build in release mode. Otherwise, produces a debug build

Specifies the file path where the built .ipa|.apk will be copied. If it points to a non-existent directory path, it will be created. If the specified value is existing directory, the original file name will be used
Specifies the directory where you want to create the project, if different from the current directory
Skips the application compatibility checks and forces npm i to ensure all dependencies are installed
Forces rebuilding the native application
Specifies a connected device/emulator to start and run the app

Allows passing additional App Components for android

Specifies the file path to the keystore file (P12) which you want to use to code sign your APK

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Installs all dependencies described in a valid package.json or installs a selected NativeScript development module as a dev dependency

subcommands.description: Enable TypeScript support

options.description: Specifies the directory which contains the package.json file, if different from the current directory

args.name: module
args.description: Specifies a NativeScript development module by path to a local directory containing a valid npm module or by name in the npm registry

subcommands.name: appstore
subcommands.description: Lists all application records in iTunes Connect. The list contains name, version and bundle ID for each application record

subcommands.name: upload
subcommands.description: Uploads project to iTunes Connect. The command either issues a production build and uploads it to iTunes Connect, or uses an already built package to upload

options.description: Use the provided .ipa file instead of building the project
Specifies the password for accessing the information you store in iTunes Transporter application
The session that will be used instead of triggering a new login each time NativeScript CLI communicates with Apple's APIs

args.name: appleId
args.description: Apple id for logging in iTunes Connect

args.name: password
args.description: Password for logging in iTunes Connect

args.name: mobile provisioning profile identifier
args.description: The identifier of the mobile provision(e.g. d5d40f61-b303-4fc8-aea3-fbb229a8171c) which will be used for building. This can easily be acquired through the iPhone Configuration Utility

args.name: code sign identity
args.description: The code sign identity which will be used for building. You can set it to something generic like 'iPhone Distribution' to let the build automatically detect a code sign identity

options.description: Specifies the team id for which Xcode will try to find distribution certificate and provisioning profile when exporting for AppStore submission
Show help for the appstore subcommand

args.name: appleId
args.description: Credentials for logging in iTunes Connect. If you do not provide them when running the command, the NativeScript CLI will prompt you to provide them

args.name: password
